title: Implement isolated browser execution

environment.os: Windows local; clean-checkout GitHub Actions pending

commands.name: Missing isolated-browser runner
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts --project property tests/property/isolated-browser-runner.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: All three focused suites failed to import the not-yet-created isolated-browser runner module

commands.name: Initial trace schema did not compile
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts --project property tests/property/isolated-browser-runner.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts
commands.expectedFailure: The first implementation had an incomplete trace-schema closure and failed during source transformation

commands.name: Strict policy configuration and seeded resource-isolation invariants
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts --project property tests/property/isolated-browser-runner.property.test.ts

commands.name: WebSocket and credential-bearing URL policy gap
commands.expectedFailure: The request-policy example rejected authorized WebSocket traffic as an unsupported protocol before explicit WebSocket and URL-credential decisions existed

commands.name: Credential-free HTTP and WebSocket request policy
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts && pnpm --filter @pactwire/runner typecheck

commands.name: Real Chromium isolation, egress, local-capability, concurrency, and crash cases
Undefined isolated-browser user stories
commands.expectedFailure: Both RUN-01 scenarios exposed twelve undefined isolated-browser step instances before the real fixture and Chromium bindings existed

commands.name: Sequential isolation and crash-recovery browser stories
Root lint rejected unscoped browser globals in BDD bindings
commands.expectedFailure: ESLint reported four no-undef violations for document references inside Playwright evaluation callbacks

commands.name: Evidence traceability required the RUN-01 manifest
commands.expectedFailure: The evidence checker reported the new unit, property, and integration browser-runner proof files as orphans

commands.name: Focused isolated-browser behavior matrix
commands.command: pnpm exec vitest run --project unit tests/unit/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts --project property tests/property/isolated-browser-runner.property.test.ts --project integration tests/integration/isolated-browser-runner.test.ts

commands.name: Root lint and evidence reconciliation
commands.command: pnpm lint && pnpm evidence:check && pnpm exec vitest run --project integration tests/integration/evidence-harness.test.ts

commands.name: Production dependency and credential-pattern audit
commands.command: pnpm audit --prod and workspace credential-pattern scan

scenarios.scenario: Sequential authorized runs receive separate local browser resources
A crashed renderer releases its run before clean recovery

visualEvidence.rationale: Source-bound optimized-production isolation and crash-recovery traces are pending the implementation source commit and clean-checkout CI; RUN-01 adds no visible product error surface that would make a screenshot applicable.

proofs.description: Strict session policy, permanent resource registry, per-run Chromium lifecycle, lower-layer request and WebSocket gates, isolated local capabilities, cleanup, diagnostics, and sanitized trace schema
Exact-origin and host policy, URL-credential and protocol refusal, strict configuration, and permanent resource non-reuse examples
Seeded PROP-20 run-order, concurrency, release, and permanent non-reassignment invariants
Real Chromium sequential and concurrent isolation, HTTP/WebSocket egress, popup, clipboard, download, renderer-crash, cleanup, and recovery checks
User-visible sequential isolation story tagged RUN-01, FR-030, and PROP-20
User-visible renderer crash and clean recovery story tagged RUN-01, FR-030, and PROP-20
Controlled fictional fixture, real per-run Chromium resources, crash injection, terminal-resource assertions, and sanitized trace capture

knownLimitations: RUN-01 exposes the isolated execution primitive to trusted runner code; RUN-03 still owns the policy-bounded GPT-5.6 computer-use adapter and ensures the model never receives raw Playwright authority.
RUN-02 still owns independent deterministic observation, capture-gap classification, and recorder-version evidence.

The network boundary is host based and enforced below future model code at the Playwright HTTP and WebSocket routing layers; this task does not claim an operating-system container or firewall boundary.
No UI screenshot is applicable because RUN-01 adds no visible UI or isolation-error surface; its required evidence is a machine-readable lifecycle trace.
All exercised hosts, identities, markers, and downloads are controlled fictional fixtures; the passing cases do not establish safety, compliance, agreement conformity, or product effectiveness.
